name: Koulourakia (Sesame Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. Cream butter and sugar with vanilla essence until light and fluffy.
3. Beat eggs, reserving 1 tablespoon for glazing. Add the remaining eggs gradually to the butter mixture, beating well.
4. Sift dry ingredients twice. Stir into the creamed mixture alternately with milk to form a soft dough. Knead lightly until smooth. If the dough is sticky, chill for 1 hour.
5. Scatter some sesame seeds lightly on one side of a pastry board. Shape pieces of dough into thick pencil shapes and roll onto the sesame seeds to coat lightly or according to taste. Alternatively, double over the rolled dough and twist, or form into rings, figure eights, or coils.
6. Place the cookies on greased baking sheets. Glaze with the reserved egg beaten with a little milk.
7. Bake in a moderate oven for 15-20 minutes until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack and store in an airtight container.
Variation: Roll the dough in granulated sugar instead of seeds, and glaze lightly with milk.
